title: How Bernard Arnault works to keep his media outlets in line
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (4/6). Since the 1990s, the CEO of LVMH has built a small media galaxy in print and radio, which he uses to convey his ideas and tastes, but also to promote his brands.

title: United States announces nuclear cooperation pact with Saudi Arabia

France records over 5,700 excess deaths during June heatwave
subhead: The extra deaths between June 17 and July 2 this year were due to 'all causes' – none yet clearly linked to the heat, health authorities said, saying final figures would be published in the fall.

title: Kremlin propagandist Xenia Fedorova escapes EU sanctions targeting Russian figures
subhead: The French Foreign Ministry and Interior Ministry are quietly exploring alternative sanctions against the pro-Russian CNews personality, former head of Russia Today France, a channel funded by the Kremlin.

title: European Commission withdraws €2 million grant from Venice Biennale over Russian presence
subhead: The major contemporary art event had allowed Russia to return to the festival and present a pavilion there in May, before closing it to the public, sparking controversy and leading the festival's jury to resign.

title: Friterie des Nations, a local institution at the Calais Beach
subhead: Gourmet kiosks (1/6). Across France, small kiosks serving local specialties are a favorite stop for holidaymakers. In Calais, northern France, Pascal Carré and his daughter Loranne serve fries that are crisp on the outside and soft on the inside.

How social media bans for young users, now in effect in France, have spread worldwide
subhead: France has decided to ban those under 15 from major platforms such as Instagram and TikTok. The country is following a global trend that has gained momentum in recent years.

title: New monkey species identified in Lomami National Park, Democratic Republic of Congo
subhead: First photographed in 2008, this small colobus monkey with pink-orange lips has now been officially described by an international team of researchers – and may be immediately classified as 'endangered.'

title: Bernard Arnault's business-first strategy with foreign heads of state, from Trump to Putin
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (3/6). The head of LVMH, which generates 70% of its revenue outside Europe, has fostered relationships with the leaders of the markets he targets. Foremost among them is the president of the United States, whom he has courted since the 1980s.
